_meta.description: oh-my-mlip distribution manifest. The resolver (oh_my_mlip/fetch.py) reads this to map an env -> its conda-pack tarball on the Hugging Face Hub, verify integrity, and refuse to unpack on a host below the env's minimum NVIDIA driver. One HF repo per env.

<env>.env: conda env name; matches models.json '<framework>'.env and the unpack dir under ${OH_MY_MLIP_HOME}/envs/<env>
<env>.hf_repo: Hugging Face Hub repo id hosting the conda-pack tarball (one repo per env)
<env>.revision: pinned HF revision (tag/commit) for reproducibility; never 'main'
<env>.sha256: sha256 of the tarball; the resolver verifies this after download before unpack
<env>.unpack_size_bytes: on-disk size after conda-unpack, for free-space precheck (integer bytes)
<env>.min_driver_version: minimum CUDA driver version the env's torch build requires; when recorded, the resolver compares it against the host driver (torch._C._cuda_getDriverVersion) and prints the install.sh fallback command if the host is lower. While it carries the 'TODO-on-upload' placeholder the comparison is skipped.

_meta.note: Seeded for the v1-shipped envs only (MACE, SevenNet). sha256 / unpack_size_bytes / min_driver_version are filled by scripts/build_conda_pack.sh + scripts/publish_hf.py at upload time; until then they carry the literal 'TODO-on-upload' marker and the resolver must treat such entries as not-yet-publishable.
